rag_rules: When fixing type mismatches in AGREE contracts, ensure left and right sides of binary expressions have identical types.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid unresolved reference errors.
generic_rules: AGREE type declarations must use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or undefined scalar types.
In AGREE contracts, guarantees must relate visible inputs to visible outputs without referencing implementation-only paths.
Assignments in implementation contracts must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Fix root type mismatches by aligning helper declaration types with their expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
AGREE constants and equations must declare types explicitly using int, real, or bool.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use component type annexes for such contracts.
